question: Are the local Ascend 910C runs and current AMD MI350 runs using the same reduced DeepSeek-V4 model config?
answer: They are comparable at the reduced 4-layer DeepSeek-V4-Flash model-identity level, but they are not identical full training/run configs.

minor_model_contract_difference.read: This is not text-identical. In the AMD local builder, compress_ratio=0 disables the compressed sparse path, while Ascend uses compress_ratio=1 for the no-compression first two layers. Treat this as intended to be semantically comparable for the first two layers, not as byte-identical configuration.

hardware_parallelism.ascend: 16 logical Ascend 910C dies, expert_parallel_degree=16, tensor_parallel_degree=1, pipeline_parallel_degree=1, context_parallel_degree=1, data_parallel_replicate_degree=1, data_parallel_shard_degree=-1

compile_and_operator_stack.ascend: torch.compile model/loss with CANN custom converters and AutoFuse ops including deepseek_v4_sfa
compile_and_operator_stack.amd_best_4x: TorchTitan WIP with MORI/AITER MoE path and prototype shared-KV sparse MLA backward modes

checkpoint_and_data.ascend: loads HF assets from /data/models/DeepSeek-V4-bf16 and uses c4_test
checkpoint_and_data.amd_best_4x: direct canary run artifacts report synthetic/small-token canary path; checkpointing disabled for the best no-AC throughput runs

comparison_read: The previous TFLOPs/token comparison is fair only as a best-effort reduced-model systems comparison, not as a strict same-config benchmark. Architecture-level dimensions and the 4-layer DSv4 Flash identity match closely enough to compare directionally. The strongest AMD 4x number is not the same run contract as Ascend because it changes activation checkpointing, EP degree/world size, GA/GBS, compiler stack, sparse-attention implementation, and communication/runtime backend. For a stricter apples-to-apples result, run AMD with full activation checkpointing and matching 4-layer config at the memory-equivalent GBS64 shape, and run Ascend or AMD with the same no-AC policy if memory permits.
